name: Shan Lin Xi Oolong
region: Shan Lin Xi, Nantou County, Taiwan
terroir: High elevation (~1600m), tea trees grow interspersed with cedar and cypress forest, cool misty climate
flavor_profile: Clean, crisp, and distinctly cool-toned — a signature 'cold aroma' from the surrounding cedar forest. White-flower florals with a backbone of alpine freshness, hints of vanilla, and a bright mineral finish. Less creamy than Ali Shan or Li Shan, more structured and refreshing.
tasting_notes: You taste the cedar forest before you taste the tea. Shan Lin Xi is grown among cedar and cypress at around 1,600m, and whatever the mechanism — root uptake, airborne terpenes, microclimate — the cup reliably carries a cool, woody aroma that no other gao shan reproduces. Where Ali Shan and Li Shan lean creamy and thick, Shan Lin Xi goes the other way: structured, crisp, almost alpine, with white-flower florals and a mineral finish that reads clean rather than rich. Later steeps drift toward wheatgrass and ground cherry. Keep the water cooler than instinct tells you — 85–90°C, no higher — because pushing past that flattens the cedar note, which is exactly what you came for. Standard 5g, patient first steep to unroll the pellets, and don't over-leaf: this is a tea that rewards restraint and punishes a crammed gaiwan. Best drunk in cool weather when the cold aroma has something to play against.

brewing.tips: Standard gao shan parameters. The cedar-forest character comes through best at 85–90°C — going hotter can flatten the distinctive cool aroma.
